title: Tries (Prefix Trees)
analogy: A Trie is like an autocomplete dictionary — each letter you type narrows down the branch you're on, until you reach the end of a word.

overview.summary: A Trie is a tree-like data structure optimized for storing and searching strings, where each node represents a character and paths from root to leaf spell out stored words.
learningObjectives: Understand the Trie node structure with children map and end-of-word flag.
Implement insert, search, and startsWith operations.
Recognize when Trie outperforms hash-based string storage.
overview.interviewRelevance: Tries appear in autocomplete, spell-check, IP routing, and word-game problems.

paragraphs: Every time your phone autocompletes a word, it's walking down a Trie. DNS lookup tables use Tries for efficient domain resolution.
Search engines use Trie-based structures to suggest completions as you type each character.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: A Trie lets you find all words starting with a prefix in O(prefix length) time, regardless of how many words are stored.

definition.technicalDefinition: A Trie (pronounced 'try') is a tree-like data structure where each node represents a character. The root is empty, and each path from root to a marked node spells out a stored word.

vocabulary.definition: A node containing a children map and an is_end flag.
A leading substring that may or may not be a complete word.
A boolean flag marking whether the path to this node forms a complete word.

keyProperties: Insert, search, and startsWith all take O(m) time where m = word length.
Space: O(m × n) where n = number of words.
Shared prefixes are stored once, making it space-efficient for common prefixes.

operations.name: Insert
operations.description: Walk down the Trie character by character, creating new nodes as needed, then mark the final node.
operations.visualNote: Characters branch out from root, shared prefixes merge into single paths.

operations.name: Search
operations.description: Walk down the Trie character by character. Return true only if the entire word exists and the final node is marked.
operations.visualNote: Path lights up green for found characters, stops red at missing character.

practice.title: Implement Trie
practice.keyInsight: Build the basic Trie with insert, search, and startsWith.

practice.title: Word Search II
practice.keyInsight: Build a Trie from the word list, then DFS the board against the Trie.

practice.title: Design Add and Search Words
practice.keyInsight: Trie with wildcard DFS for '.' characters.

practice.title: Replace Words
practice.keyInsight: Store roots in a Trie, replace words with shortest matching root.

conceptQuiz.question: What is the time complexity of searching for a word of length m in a Trie?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Search follows m edges, one per character, regardless of total words stored.

conceptQuiz.question: What advantage does a Trie have over a hash set for string storage?
options: Lower memory usage for all cases
conceptQuiz.explanation: Tries excel at prefix-based operations like autocomplete, which hash sets cannot do efficiently.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Given a dictionary of words, implement an autocomplete system that returns all words starting with a given prefix.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: Prefix-based queries are the core strength of Tries.
